owasp_top10: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
mitre_attack: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
T1195.002 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
cwe: CWE-345 - Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity
CWE-347 - Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
CWE-353 - Missing Support for Integrity Check

threat.title: Git Commit Signature Bypass and Repository Tampering
threat.description: When Git repositories do not enforce GPG/SSH commit signature verification, attackers can inject malicious code through unsigned commits, force-push history rewrites, or spoof commit authorship. This enables supply chain attacks by compromising the integrity of source code at the repository level, potentially affecting millions of downstream users.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker gains push access to repository through compromised credentials or insider access
2. Disables local GPG signing (git config --global commit.gpgsign false)
3. Crafts malicious commit with spoofed author identity (git commit --author="Trusted Dev <dev@company.com>")
4. Pushes unsigned commit to main branch or creates seemingly legitimate pull request
5. CI/CD pipeline automatically builds and deploys the malicious code
6. Backdoor is distributed to production systems and downstream users
7. Attacker uses force-push (git push --force) to rewrite history and remove evidence
prerequisites: Repository does not require signed commits via branch protection rules
No server-side pre-receive hooks validating commit signatures
Attacker has write access to repository (compromised credentials, insider threat)
CI/CD pipeline lacks signature verification in build process
No commit signature audit logging or monitoring

cve_references.description: Git credential storage vulnerability allowing credential theft for repository access
Git command injection via malicious repository configuration enabling code execution
Git remote code execution via crafted repository on case-insensitive filesystems

real_world_breaches.incident: Linux Kernel Git Server Compromise Attempt

real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attackers gained access to kernel.org servers and attempted to modify Git repositories without signature verification enforcement

real_world_breaches.impact: 3 million weekly downloads compromised; malware injected via GitHub repository takeover
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attacker gained access to maintainer's GitHub account and pushed unsigned malicious commits to popular npm package

real_world_breaches.impact: Thousands of customers exposed; attacker modified Git-hosted Bash script for 2 months
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unsigned commits allowed attacker to inject credential-stealing code into Codecov's Docker image creation process

real_world_breaches.impact: 18,000+ organizations compromised including US government agencies; $100M+ in damages
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attackers compromised build system and injected malicious code into Git repository without detection via unsigned commits

real_world_breaches.impact: Researchers demonstrated ability to spoof verified commit badges through email manipulation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: GitHub's GPG signature verification relied on email matching without proper key ownership validation

bug_bounty_reports.summary: GitHub Enterprise commit signature bypass allowing unsigned commits in protected branches
GitLab push rules bypass enabling force-push to protected branches without signature verification

security_research.title: Signing Git Commits - A Supply Chain Security Necessity
security_research.key_finding: Only 12% of open-source projects require commit signature verification, leaving 88% vulnerable to tampering

security_research.title: Supply Chain Security: Compromising Package Managers
security_research.key_finding: Git repository tampering is the #2 supply chain attack vector after dependency confusion

security_research.title: NIST SSDF: Secure Software Development Framework
security_research.key_finding: PW.4.4: Protect all forms of code from unauthorized access and tampering - requires commit signing

mitigations.title: Enforce GPG/SSH Signed Commits via GitHub Branch Protection
mitigations.description: Require all commits to be cryptographically signed with GPG or SSH keys before they can be pushed to protected branches. This ensures commit authenticity and prevents unauthorized code injection.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Step 1: Generate GPG key for commit signing
gpg --full-generate-key
# Select: (1) RSA and RSA, 4096 bits, no expiration
# Use your GitHub email address

# Step 2: List GPG keys and copy key ID
gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
# Output: sec   rsa4096/ABCD1234EFGH5678 2024-01-30 [SC]
# Key ID is: ABCD1234EFGH5678

# Step 3: Configure Git to use GPG key
git config --global user.signingkey ABCD1234EFGH5678
git config --global commit.gpgsign true
git config --global tag.gpgsign true

# Step 4: Export public key and add to GitHub
gpg --armor --export ABCD1234EFGH5678
# Copy output and paste into GitHub Settings > SSH and GPG keys > New GPG key

# Step 5: Test signed commit
echo "test" > test.txt
git add test.txt
git commit -S -m "Test signed commit"
# Verify signature
git log --show-signature -1

# Step 6: Enable branch protection with signature requirement (GitHub Settings)
# Go to: Repository Settings > Branches > Branch protection rules > Add rule
# Enable: "Require signed commits"

# GitHub API method to enable programmatically:
curl -X PUT \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
  -H "Authorization: token ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \
  https://api.github.com/repos/OWNER/REPO/branches/main/protection \
  -d '{
    "required_status_checks": null,
    "enforce_admins": true,
    "required_pull_request_reviews": null,
    "restrictions": null,
    "required_signatures": true
  }'

# Step 7: Verify protection is active
curl -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
  -H "Authorization: token ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \
  https://api.github.com/repos/OWNER/REPO/branches/main/protection/required_signatures

# Alternative: SSH commit signing (GitHub, Git 2.34+)
# Generate Ed25519 SSH key
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"

# Configure Git to use SSH signing
git config --global gpg.format ssh
git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

# Add SSH key to GitHub as signing key
# GitHub Settings > SSH and GPG keys > New SSH key > Key type: Signing Key

nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms verify software, firmware, and information integrity
PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle to manage systems is implemented
validation_steps: Verify GPG key is generated and configured: git config --global --get user.signingkey
Test commit signing: git log --show-signature -1
Attempt unsigned commit push to protected branch (should fail)
Verify GitHub branch protection status via API or UI
Check commit badges show 'Verified' in GitHub UI

mitigations.title: Implement Server-Side Pre-Receive Hooks for Signature Validation
mitigations.description: Deploy Git server-side hooks to validate commit signatures before accepting pushes. This provides defense-in-depth even if client-side signing is bypassed.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# File: /path/to/repo.git/hooks/pre-receive
# This hook runs on the server before updating refs

set -e

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Read protected branches from config
PROTECTED_BRANCHES=("main" "master" "production" "release/*")

# Read stdin: old-value new-value ref-name
while read oldrev newrev refname; do
    # Extract branch name
    branch=$(echo "$refname" | sed 's|refs/heads/||')
    
    # Check if branch is protected
    is_protected=false
    for pattern in "${PROTECTED_BRANCHES[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$branch" == $pattern ]]; then
            is_protected=true
            break
        fi
    done
    
    if [ "$is_protected" = false ]; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} Branch '$branch' is not protected, allowing push"
        continue
    fi
    
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠${NC} Validating signatures for protected branch: $branch"
    
    # Get list of commits being pushed
    if [ "$oldrev" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
        # New branch - check all commits
        commits=$(git rev-list "$newrev")
    else
        # Existing branch - check new commits only
        commits=$(git rev-list "${oldrev}..${newrev}")
    fi
    
    # Validate each commit signature
    unsigned_commits=()
    invalid_signatures=()
    
    for commit in $commits; do
        # Check if commit is signed
        if ! git verify-commit --raw "$commit" 2>&1 | grep -q "GOODSIG\|VALIDSIG"; then
            # Check if commit has any signature at all
            if ! git cat-file commit "$commit" | grep -q "^gpgsig "; then
                unsigned_commits+=("$commit")
                author=$(git log -1 --format='%an <%ae>' "$commit")
                message=$(git log -1 --format='%s' "$commit")
                echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} Unsigned commit: $commit"
                echo -e "  Author: $author"
                echo -e "  Message: $message"
            else
                invalid_signatures+=("$commit")
                echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} Invalid signature: $commit"
            fi
        else
            # Valid signature - verify signing key is authorized
            signing_key=$(git verify-commit --raw "$commit" 2>&1 | grep "VALIDSIG" | awk '{print $3}')
            
            # Load authorized keys from file
            authorized_keys_file="/etc/git/authorized_signing_keys.txt"
            
            if [ -f "$authorized_keys_file" ]; then
                if ! grep -q "$signing_key" "$authorized_keys_file"; then
                    echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} Commit $commit signed with unauthorized key: $signing_key"
                    invalid_signatures+=("$commit")
                    continue
                fi
            fi
            
            echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} Valid signature: $commit (key: ${signing_key:0:8}...)"
        fi
    done
    
    # Reject push if any commits are unsigned or have invalid signatures
    if [ ${#unsigned_commits[@]} -gt 0 ] || [ ${#invalid_signatures[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
        echo -e "${RED}PUSH REJECTED: Signature Verification Failed${NC}"
        echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
        echo ""
        
        if [ ${#unsigned_commits[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
            echo -e "${RED}Unsigned commits detected: ${#unsigned_commits[@]}${NC}"
            echo "All commits to '$branch' must be signed."
            echo ""
            echo "To sign your commits:"
            echo "  1. Configure GPG: git config --global commit.gpgsign true"
            echo "  2. Set signing key: git config --global user.signingkey YOUR_KEY_ID"
            echo "  3. Sign existing commits: git rebase --exec 'git commit --amend --no-edit -S' HEAD~N"
        fi
        
        if [ ${#invalid_signatures[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
            echo -e "${RED}Invalid signatures detected: ${#invalid_signatures[@]}${NC}"
            echo "Signatures must be made with authorized GPG keys."
            echo "Contact your administrator to add your key to the authorized list."
        fi
        
        echo ""
        exit 1
    fi
    
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All commits properly signed, accepting push to $branch${NC}"
done

exit 0

# Installation:
# 1. Save as /path/to/repo.git/hooks/pre-receive
# 2. chmod +x /path/to/repo.git/hooks/pre-receive
# 3. Create authorized keys file: /etc/git/authorized_signing_keys.txt
# 4. Add GPG key IDs (one per line) to authorized_signing_keys.txt

# For GitHub Enterprise / GitLab:
# Deploy as organization-wide custom hook
# GitHub: /data/user/common/pre-receive-hook.d/
# GitLab: /opt/gitlab/embedded/service/gitlab-shell/hooks/pre-receive.d/

nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms verify software
validation_steps: Deploy pre-receive hook to test repository
Attempt to push unsigned commit (should be rejected)
Attempt to push commit with invalid signature (should be rejected)
Push properly signed commit (should succeed)
Verify hook logs are generated and monitored
Test hook performance with large commit batches

mitigations.title: Enable Comprehensive Git Audit Logging and Monitoring
mitigations.description: Implement centralized logging of all Git operations including push events, branch protection changes, and signature verification failures to detect tampering attempts.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Git Audit Logging with SIEM Integration

# 1. Enable GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) audit log streaming
# GitHub Enterprise Cloud: Settings > Audit log > Streaming
# Configure Azure Event Hubs, Splunk HEC, or AWS S3

# 2. Configure GitHub audit log webhook
curl -X POST \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
  -H "Authorization: token ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" \
  https://api.github.com/orgs/ORG_NAME/hooks \
  -d '{
    "name": "web",
    "active": true,
    "events": [
      "push",
      "repository",
      "member",
      "organization",
      "team",
      "branch_protection_rule"
    ],
    "config": {
      "url": "https://your-siem.com/github-webhook",
      "content_type": "json",
      "secret": "your-webhook-secret"
    }
  }'

# 3. GitLab audit log export (Premium/Ultimate)
# File: /etc/gitlab/gitlab.rb
# audit_events_streaming_destinations = [
#   {
#     destination_url: "https://splunk.example.com:8088/services/collector",
#     verification_token: "splunk-hec-token"
#   }
# ]

# 4. Custom Git hook for local audit logging
# File: /path/to/repo.git/hooks/post-receive
cat > post-receive << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

# Audit log configuration
AUDIT_LOG_FILE="/var/log/git-audit/$(basename $(pwd)).log"
SIEM_ENDPOINT="https://siem.company.com/api/events"
SIEM_API_KEY="${SIEM_API_KEY}"

# Create log directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p $(dirname "$AUDIT_LOG_FILE")

# Read push information
while read oldrev newrev refname; do
    branch=$(echo "$refname" | sed 's|refs/heads/||')
    
    # Get commits
    if [ "$oldrev" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
        commits=$(git rev-list "$newrev" --max-count=10)
    else
        commits=$(git rev-list "${oldrev}..${newrev}")
    fi
    
    # Analyze each commit
    for commit in $commits; do
        author=$(git log -1 --format='%an' "$commit")
        author_email=$(git log -1 --format='%ae' "$commit")
        committer=$(git log -1 --format='%cn' "$commit")
        committer_email=$(git log -1 --format='%ce' "$commit")
        message=$(git log -1 --format='%s' "$commit")
        timestamp=$(git log -1 --format='%aI' "$commit")
        
        # Check signature status
        signature_status="unsigned"
        signing_key=""
        
        if git verify-commit --raw "$commit" 2>&1 | grep -q "GOODSIG\|VALIDSIG"; then
            signature_status="valid"
            signing_key=$(git verify-commit --raw "$commit" 2>&1 | grep "VALIDSIG" | awk '{print $3}')
        elif git cat-file commit "$commit" | grep -q "^gpgsig "; then
            signature_status="invalid"
        fi
        
        # Check for suspicious patterns
        suspicion_score=0
        alerts=()
        
        # Author/committer mismatch
        if [ "$author_email" != "$committer_email" ]; then
            suspicion_score=$((suspicion_score + 2))
            alerts+=("author_committer_mismatch")
        fi
        
        # Unsigned commit
        if [ "$signature_status" = "unsigned" ]; then
            suspicion_score=$((suspicion_score + 3))
            alerts+=("unsigned_commit")
        fi
        
        # Force push indicator
        if [ "$oldrev" != "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
            if ! git merge-base --is-ancestor "$oldrev" "$newrev"; then
                suspicion_score=$((suspicion_score + 5))
                alerts+=("force_push_detected")
            fi
        fi
        
        # Suspicious commit messages
        if echo "$message" | grep -qiE "(test|temp|wip|hack|bypass|disable)"; then
            suspicion_score=$((suspicion_score + 1))
            alerts+=("suspicious_message")
        fi
        
        # Build JSON audit event
        event_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "event_type": "git.push",
  "repository": "$(basename $(pwd))",
  "branch": "$branch",
  "commit": {
    "sha": "$commit",
    "author": "$author",
    "author_email": "$author_email",
    "committer": "$committer",
    "committer_email": "$committer_email",
    "message": "$message",
    "timestamp": "$timestamp",
    "signature_status": "$signature_status",
    "signing_key": "$signing_key"
  },
  "security": {
    "suspicion_score": $suspicion_score,
    "alerts": $(printf '%s\n' "${alerts[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .)
  },
  "user": {
    "ip_address": "${SSH_CONNECTION%% *}",
    "username": "$USER"
  }
}
JSON
)
        
        # Write to local audit log
        echo "$event_json" >> "$AUDIT_LOG_FILE"
        
        # Send to SIEM
        if [ -n "$SIEM_ENDPOINT" ] && [ -n "$SIEM_API_KEY" ]; then
            curl -s -X POST "$SIEM_ENDPOINT" \
              -H "Authorization: Bearer $SIEM_API_KEY" \
              -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
              -d "$event_json" &> /dev/null
        fi
        
        # Alert on high suspicion score
        if [ $suspicion_score -ge 5 ]; then
            logger -t git-audit -p auth.warning "[SECURITY] High-risk Git push detected: $commit in $(basename $(pwd)):$branch (score: $suspicion_score)"
        fi
    done
done

exit 0
EOF

chmod +x post-receive

# 5. Configure log rotation
cat > /etc/logrotate.d/git-audit << 'EOF'
/var/log/git-audit/*.log {
    daily
    rotate 90
    compress
    delaycompress
    missingok
    notifempty
    create 0640 git git
}
EOF

nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
DE.CM-1 - Network is monitored to detect anomalous events
PR.PT-1 - Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed
validation_steps: Enable audit logging in GitHub/GitLab settings
Configure SIEM integration endpoint
Perform test push and verify log ingestion
Create SIEM dashboard for Git security events
Test alerting on unsigned commits and force pushes
Verify log retention meets compliance requirements (90 days minimum)

mitigations.title: Implement CI/CD Pipeline Commit Signature Verification
mitigations.description: Add signature verification as a mandatory CI/CD pipeline step to prevent building and deploying code from unsigned or tampered commits.

code_example.code:
# GitHub Actions workflow
# File: .github/workflows/verify-signatures.yml
name: Verify Commit Signatures

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop, release/* ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]

jobs:
  verify-signatures:
    name: Verify All Commit Signatures
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # Fetch all history for signature verification
      
      - name: Import trusted GPG keys
        run: |
          # Import organization's trusted signing keys
          mkdir -p ~/.gnupg
          chmod 700 ~/.gnupg
          echo "${{ secrets.TRUSTED_GPG_KEYS }}" | base64 -d | gpg --import
          
          # Alternative: Download from key server
          # gpg --keyserver keys.openpgp.org --recv-keys KEY_ID_1 KEY_ID_2
      
      - name: Verify commit signatures
        run: |
          set -e
          
          echo "Verifying commit signatures..."
          
          # Get list of commits to verify
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            # PR: verify commits in PR
            COMMITS=$(git rev-list ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}..${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }})
          else
            # Push: verify commits in push
            COMMITS=$(git rev-list ${{ github.event.before }}..${{ github.event.after }})
          fi
          
          if [ -z "$COMMITS" ]; then
            echo "No commits to verify"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          unsigned_count=0
          invalid_count=0
          
          for commit in $COMMITS; do
            author=$(git log -1 --format='%an <%ae>' $commit)
            message=$(git log -1 --format='%s' $commit)
            
            echo ""
            echo "Checking commit: $commit"
            echo "Author: $author"
            echo "Message: $message"
            
            # Check if commit has a signature
            if ! git cat-file commit $commit | grep -q "^gpgsig "; then
              echo "❌ UNSIGNED COMMIT"
              unsigned_count=$((unsigned_count + 1))
              continue
            fi
            
            # Verify signature
            if git verify-commit $commit 2>&1 | grep -qE "Good signature|gpg: Good signature"; then
              signing_key=$(git log -1 --format='%GK' $commit)
              signer=$(git log -1 --format='%GS' $commit)
              echo "✅ Valid signature by $signer (key: ${signing_key:0:16}...)"
            else
              echo "❌ INVALID SIGNATURE"
              invalid_count=$((invalid_count + 1))
            fi
          done
          
          echo ""
          echo "========================================"
          echo "Signature Verification Summary"
          echo "========================================"
          echo "Total commits: $(echo "$COMMITS" | wc -l)"
          echo "Unsigned commits: $unsigned_count"
          echo "Invalid signatures: $invalid_count"
          
          if [ $unsigned_count -gt 0 ] || [ $invalid_count -gt 0 ]; then
            echo ""
            echo "❌ VERIFICATION FAILED"
            echo "All commits must be signed with a trusted GPG key."
            echo ""
            echo "To sign your commits:"
            echo "  git config --global commit.gpgsign true"
            echo "  git config --global user.signingkey YOUR_KEY_ID"
            echo ""
            exit 1
          fi
          
          echo "✅ All commits properly signed"
      
      - name: Post verification status
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const status = '${{ job.status }}';
            const message = status === 'success' 
              ? '✅ All commits are properly signed'
              : '❌ Signature verification failed - see workflow logs';
            
            if (context.eventName === 'pull_request') {
              github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body: `## Commit Signature Verification\n\n${message}`
              });
            }

---

# GitLab CI Pipeline
# File: .gitlab-ci.yml
verify-signatures:
  stage: validate
  image: alpine:latest
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache git gnupg
    - mkdir -p ~/.gnupg
    - chmod 700 ~/.gnupg
    # Import trusted keys
    - echo "$TRUSTED_GPG_KEYS" | base64 -d | gpg --import
  script:
    - |
      echo "Verifying commit signatures..."
      
      # Get commits to verify
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TARGET_BRANCH_NAME" ]; then
        # Merge request
        COMMITS=$(git rev-list origin/${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TARGET_BRANCH_NAME}..HEAD)
      else
        # Push event
        COMMITS=$(git rev-list ${CI_COMMIT_BEFORE_SHA}..${CI_COMMIT_SHA})
      fi
      
      unsigned_count=0
      invalid_count=0
      
      for commit in $COMMITS; do
        echo "Verifying $commit"
        
        if ! git cat-file commit $commit | grep -q "^gpgsig "; then
          echo "ERROR: Unsigned commit $commit"
          unsigned_count=$((unsigned_count + 1))
        elif ! git verify-commit $commit 2>&1 | grep -q "Good signature"; then
          echo "ERROR: Invalid signature on $commit"
          invalid_count=$((invalid_count + 1))
        else
          echo "OK: Valid signature on $commit"
        fi
      done
      
      if [ $unsigned_count -gt 0 ] || [ $invalid_count -gt 0 ]; then
        echo "FAILURE: Found $unsigned_count unsigned and $invalid_count invalid signatures"
        exit 1
      fi
      
      echo "SUCCESS: All commits properly signed"
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main" || $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"'

---

# Azure DevOps Pipeline
# File: azure-pipelines.yml
trigger:
  branches:
    include:
      - main
      - develop

pool:
  vmImage: 'ubuntu-latest'

steps:
- checkout: self
  fetchDepth: 0

- bash: |
    set -e
    
    # Install GPG
    sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y gnupg
    
    # Import trusted keys
    echo "$TRUSTED_GPG_KEYS" | base64 -d | gpg --import
    
    # Verify signatures
    COMMITS=$(git rev-list $(Build.SourceVersion)^..$(Build.SourceVersion))
    
    for commit in $COMMITS; do
      if ! git verify-commit $commit; then
        echo "##vso[task.logissue type=error]Commit $commit signature verification failed"
        exit 1
      fi
    done
    
    echo "All commits properly signed"
  displayName: 'Verify Commit Signatures'
  env:
    TRUSTED_GPG_KEYS: $(trusted-gpg-keys)

tools.name: GitHub Actions
tools.cost: Free for public repos; $0.008/minute for private

nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms verify software
PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
validation_steps: Add signature verification workflow to repository
Configure trusted GPG keys as CI/CD secrets
Test with signed commit (should pass)
Test with unsigned commit (should fail)
Verify workflow prevents merging unsigned commits
Check pipeline execution time (should add <30 seconds)

indicators: Unsigned commits pushed to protected branches
GPG signature verification failures in server logs
Commits from unknown or unauthorized GPG keys
Branch protection rules disabled or modified
Commits with suspicious messages (test, temp, bypass, disable)
After-hours commits from unusual geographic locations
Commits modifying security-critical files without review

log_queries.query:
// Detect unsigned commits pushed to protected branches
let ProtectedBranches = dynamic(['main', 'master', 'production']);
GitHubAuditLog
| where Action == 'git.push'
| extend Branch = tostring(parse_json(Data).ref)
| where Branch has_any (ProtectedBranches)
| extend CommitSigned = tostring(parse_json(Data).commit_signed)
| where CommitSigned == 'false' or isempty(CommitSigned)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Repository, Branch, CommitSHA = tostring(parse_json(Data).commit_sha), IPAddress
| summarize UnsignedCommitCount = count() by Actor, Repository, Branch, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where UnsignedCommitCount > 0
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Detect force-push operations indicating history rewriting
GitHubAuditLog
| where Action == 'git.push'
| extend ForcePush = tostring(parse_json(Data).forced)
| where ForcePush == 'true'
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Repository, Branch = tostring(parse_json(Data).ref), 
  OldSHA = tostring(parse_json(Data).before), NewSHA = tostring(parse_json(Data).after), IPAddress
| join kind=leftouter (
    GeoIPMapping  // Custom table mapping IPs to countries
  ) on IPAddress
| extend RiskScore = case(
    Country !in ('US', 'CA', 'GB'),  // Trusted countries
    5,
    3
  )
| where RiskScore >= 3
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=github sourcetype=github:audit action="git.push"
| eval branch=spath(_raw, "data.ref")
| search branch IN ("refs/heads/main", "refs/heads/master", "refs/heads/production")
| eval commit_signed=spath(_raw, "data.commit_signed")
| where commit_signed="false" OR isnull(commit_signed)
| eval commit_sha=spath(_raw, "data.commit_sha")
| table _time, actor, repository, branch, commit_sha, src_ip
| stats count as unsigned_commits by actor, repository
| where unsigned_commits > 0
| sort -unsigned_commits

index=github sourcetype=github:audit action="protected_branch.*"
| eval action_type=case(
    action="protected_branch.disabled", "DISABLED",
    action="protected_branch.destroy", "DELETED",
    action="protected_branch.update", "MODIFIED",
    1=1, action
  )
| eval required_signatures=spath(_raw, "data.required_signatures")
| where required_signatures="false" OR action_type IN ("DISABLED", "DELETED")
| table _time, actor, repository, action_type, branch, required_signatures
| eval severity="CRITICAL"
| outputlookup append=true github_security_alerts.csv

event.dataset: "github.audit" AND event.action: "git.push" AND 
github.repo_protection.branch: ("main" OR "master" OR "production") AND 
(NOT github.commit.signed: true OR NOT _exists_: github.commit.signed)

event.dataset: "github.audit" AND event.action: "git.push" AND 
(
  github.commit.author_email: *@gmail.com OR 
  github.commit.author_email: *@outlook.com OR
  NOT github.commit.author_email: *@company.com
) AND 
github.repo_protection.branch: ("main" OR "master")

# For GitHub Enterprise Cloud audit logs streamed to CloudWatch
fields @timestamp, actor, action, repository, data.ref as branch, data.commit_signed as signed
| filter action = "git.push"
| filter branch in ["refs/heads/main", "refs/heads/master", "refs/heads/production"]
| filter signed = "false" or isempty(signed)
| stats count(*) as unsigned_commits by actor, repository
| filter unsigned_commits > 0
| sort unsigned_commits desc

# GitLab API query for unsigned commits
curl --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: <token>" \
  "https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/:id/events?action=pushed&target_type=commit" \
  | jq '.[] | select(.push_data.commit_signed == false) | 
    {author: .author.name, project: .project_id, commit: .push_data.commit_sha, 
     timestamp: .created_at}'

tools.name: GitHub Secret Scanning
tools.usage: Automatically scans for exposed secrets in commits

tools.name: Sigstore/Rekor
tools.usage: Immutable audit trail of code signatures
